reasoning: The military government of Mali, led by Assimi Goïta, has just issued a decree on May 13, 2025, strictly dissolving all political parties. Follow-up actions, such as scrapping the national Charter on Political Parties on May 14 and arresting opposition figures, demonstrate a firm commitment to this autocratic consolidation of power. The government has stated intentions to draft new laws to limit and regulate political parties, but structural reforms drafted by military juntas traditionally take many months, if not years, to be enacted. Furthermore, Mali's junta has recently insulated itself from international and regional pressures by withdrawing from ECOWAS and aligning structurally with Burkina Faso and Niger. Reversing such a consequential decree within just a month and a half (before July 1, 2025) is extremely unlikely under normal circumstances. Even factoring in the small risk of a counter-coup or sudden mass unrest forcing the junta's hand, the 40-day timeframe provides very little runway for such a dramatic sequence of events. Thus, the probability of any reversal, full or partial, before July 1, 2025, is assessed to be highly improbable.
batch_queries: Before July 1, 2025, will the government of Mali reverse its decision to dissolve all political parties?
